source_sizes.retention_pattern_waves: Wave 1-20. loop_d_1 through loop_d_286. Total: 425+ entries.

ideas.title: How I made BULLETPROOF Batman Armour
ideas.one_line_premise: I test whether different homemade materials can stop a bullet by escalating through real shots until the answer is visually undeniable at an outdoor ballistic range with the target rig and impact camera both visible, narrating every sensation in my hands until the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result.

concept.title: How I made BULLETPROOF Batman Armour
concept.logline: I test whether different homemade materials can stop a bullet by escalating through real shots until the answer is visually undeniable at an outdoor ballistic range with the target rig and impact camera both visible, narrating every sensation in my hands until the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result.
concept.promise: You're watching me test whether different homemade materials can stop a bullet by escalating through real shots until the answer is visually undeniable — the question is where different homemade materials either stops the bullet or visibly fails.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on the target rig, impact zone, and projectile test setup all framed together before the first shot. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my hands are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter an outdoor ballistic range with the target rig and impact camera both visible; a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first hands sensation narration; counter begins to tick; a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with hands + impact word: "hands's numb — insane."

first_5s: the target rig, impact zone, and projectile test setup all framed together before the first shot
hook_quarter: a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen
mid: alternate hands close-ups with a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

drivers.source: findings.kept_signals.pat_making_v2 (delta_r2=+0.012; 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M) + proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos)
top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
title_making_keyword ($24M avg for build/test format) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot before/after or build-until-fail visible payoff)

creator_fit.note: Creator-fit is added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector rewards maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level. Weights derived from indicator strengths above — no hand-curated creator taste beyond what the corpus already implies.

drivers.source: proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos) + top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot visible payoff)
wave11_12.end_begin_ratio — qualitative reveal is single-shot legible when premise copy names a concrete artifact (scored in D)

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frames ARE the proof moment)
proof_clarity.note: Proof-clarity rewards single-shot legible payoffs (build+test hybrids, body before/after anchors, numeric counter freeze + named artifact) and penalizes observation/identity endpoints with no artifact, cognitive/head-framed payoffs, and stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. Added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector and final re-rank both reward mechanism visibility at the concrete premise level. No hand-picked top-5 — every weight reads a factual premise field and cites a corpus indicator.

drivers.source: body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in premise copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

endpoint is numeric/body/build_test AND the premise has no builtin reveal — composeTitle() appends a single-frame freeze ("The Counter Froze At N" / "The Timer Hit N Exactly" / "The Mile Counter Froze At N" / "My {part} Quit First" / "The Build Held Until N"); wave11_12.end_begin_ratio
first_frame_action names an action verb + at least one of {gauge, physical object} — the challenge is legible at t=0s; HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause applied at the opening

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frame at t=0s IS comprehension)
visual_legibility.note: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No abstract classification layer — the six axes are factual premise fields plus the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

build_phases.rationale: Build phases are validated against the top-5 retention predictors + narrative_arc_analysis (best/worst arc labels) + wave7 retention-shape evidence.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

build_phases.filter: retention-curve predictors ranked by |r_with_views| across the video pool

best_arc.description: 54% of all videos follow this arc. Consistently building above_baseline from hook to end.

worst_arc.description: Front-loaded content that loses viewers progressively. 10x worse than steady_rise.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)

object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (How I made BULLETPROOF Batman Armour) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 7 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.52 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.48 from 4 drivers (+4/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

ideas.title: Walked 50,000 steps and THIS happens
ideas.one_line_premise: I walk 50,000 steps in a single push, narrating every kilometre marker and every physical change on camera until the one thing I was most worried about either happens or doesn't on a route long enough to hit 50,000 steps with a visible step counter and kilometre markers, narrating every sensation in my feet until my body does the one thing I was most worried about with the step counter still running in frame.

concept.title: Walked 50,000 steps and THIS happens
concept.logline: I walk 50,000 steps in a single push, narrating every kilometre marker and every physical change on camera until the one thing I was most worried about either happens or doesn't on a route long enough to hit 50,000 steps with a visible step counter and kilometre markers, narrating every sensation in my feet until my body does the one thing I was most worried about with the step counter still running in frame.
concept.promise: You're watching me walk 50,000 steps in a single push, narrating every kilometre marker and every physical change on camera until the one thing I was most worried about either happens or doesn't — the question is where 50,000 steps produces the one moment I was most worried about.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: my body does the one thing I was most worried about with the step counter still running in frame; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers my body does the one thing I was most worried about with the step counter still running in frame as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on the step counter at zero and my feet in motion inside the first second, both in the same frame. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my feet are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on my stride on pavement while the step counter climbs and my body narrates each new milestone for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter a route long enough to hit 50,000 steps with a visible step counter and kilometre markers; my stride on pavement while the step counter climbs and my body narrates each new milestone; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first feet sensation narration; counter begins to tick; my stride on pavement while the step counter climbs and my body narrates each new milestone
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating my stride on pavement while the step counter climbs and my body narrates each new milestone with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: my body does the one thing I was most worried about with the step counter still running in frame; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, my body does the one thing I was most worried about with the step counter still running in frame; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with feet + impact word: "feet's numb — insane."

first_5s: the step counter at zero and my feet in motion inside the first second, both in the same frame
hook_quarter: my stride on pavement while the step counter climbs and my body narrates each new milestone
mid: alternate feet close-ups with my stride on pavement while the step counter climbs and my body narrates each new milestone
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

It reads the concrete premise along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

top_indicators.quantification: Title contains making/build/creat/construct — 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M

top_indicators.why: highest-value single title-word pattern in corpus

top_indicators.quantification: Video shot in workshop-like environment (42/203 positive, 2.6x)
Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops

top_indicators.why: single-shot visible payoff structure rewards transformation/build endings

creator_fit.filter: indicators with published r/r_partial/delta_r2 in the evidence lattice above a minimum effect threshold

drivers_triggered.source: inverse of visual_is_workshop (r=+0.236); HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause favors tactile fixed framing over roaming outdoor
top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (numeric counter freeze = single-shot visible payoff)

creator_fit.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass and MMR fill both reward maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
top_indicators.why: the proof MOMENT is an action frame — visible build/test/body/counter state in one shot
single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.filter: factual premise fields × endpoint kind — each driver exposes the matched verb/anchor/artifact and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio — numeric counter freeze is single-shot legible proof
first_frame_action / visual_action_short names a physical artifact or overlay — HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause (artifact visible in the proof shot)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

top_indicators.quantification: Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops
top_indicators.why: frame-1 comprehensibility IS an action frame at t=0s — action verb in first_frame_action is the opening-second proxy for this cause
visible body_part_phrase (foot/shoulders/stomach/hand) has a surface to film; feeling/head/mind/brain/memory do not
title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

top_indicators.quantification: Title contains making/build/creat/construct — 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (Walked 50,000 steps and THIS happens) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 9 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.34 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.46 from 4 drivers (+4/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

ideas.title: I got called fat so I became a BODYBUILDER
ideas.one_line_premise: I rebuild my body like a bodybuilder and keep comparing the same poses until the before and after become impossible to argue with in the same gym and mirror setup every time so the change is undeniable, narrating every sensation in my body until the matching before/after pose cut lands and the change becomes impossible to argue with.

concept.title: I got called fat so I became a BODYBUILDER
concept.logline: I rebuild my body like a bodybuilder and keep comparing the same poses until the before and after become impossible to argue with in the same gym and mirror setup every time so the change is undeniable, narrating every sensation in my body until the matching before/after pose cut lands and the change becomes impossible to argue with.
concept.promise: You're watching me rebuild my body like a bodybuilder and keep comparing the same poses until the before and after become impossible to argue with — the question is where the before/after pose makes the change undeniable.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the matching before/after pose cut lands and the change becomes impossible to argue with; a single word of reaction lands as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers the matching before/after pose cut lands and the change becomes impossible to argue with as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on a side-by-side day-1 mirror frame locked to the exact pose I will repeat at the end. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my body is going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on matching gym reps and identical pose checks cutting against each other for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter the same gym and mirror setup every time so the change is undeniable; matching gym reps and identical pose checks cutting against each other; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first body sensation narration; counter begins to tick; matching gym reps and identical pose checks cutting against each other
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating matching gym reps and identical pose checks cutting against each other with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the matching before/after pose cut lands and the change becomes impossible to argue with; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the matching before/after pose cut lands and the change becomes impossible to argue with; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with body + impact word: "body's numb — insane."

first_5s: a side-by-side day-1 mirror frame locked to the exact pose I will repeat at the end
hook_quarter: matching gym reps and identical pose checks cutting against each other
mid: alternate body close-ups with matching gym reps and identical pose checks cutting against each other
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
tactile frame preserved without triggering TECHNICAL_MATERIAL_LANGUAGE drop cause — sensory > technical axis

creator_fit.note: Creator-fit is added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector rewards maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level. Weights derived from indicator strengths above — no hand-curated creator taste beyond what the corpus already implies.

drivers.source: endpoint is experiment/identity and no single-frame artifact is named — inverse of HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + inverse of end_begin_ratio (payoff reads only with context)
HANDS_VISIBLE match in visual_action_short / first_frame_action — indicator_registry.visual_is_workshop axis (hands-on-object framing)

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frames ARE the proof moment)
proof_clarity.note: Proof-clarity rewards single-shot legible payoffs (build+test hybrids, body before/after anchors, numeric counter freeze + named artifact) and penalizes observation/identity endpoints with no artifact, cognitive/head-framed payoffs, and stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. Added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector and final re-rank both reward mechanism visibility at the concrete premise level. No hand-picked top-5 — every weight reads a factual premise field and cites a corpus indicator.

drivers.source: body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in premise copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES
title premise line signals a single-frame payoff (same-shot / weigh-in / freeze-frame / break point / call-it moment) — wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot end-over-start reveal)
first_frame_action names an action verb + at least one of {gauge, physical object} — the challenge is legible at t=0s; HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause applied at the opening

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frame at t=0s IS comprehension)
visual_legibility.note: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No abstract classification layer — the six axes are factual premise fields plus the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.

Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

best_arc.description: 54% of all videos follow this arc. Consistently building above_baseline from hook to end.

worst_arc.description: Front-loaded content that loses viewers progressively. 10x worse than steady_rise.

climax_and_payoff.rationale: Climax & final-5% payoff is validated against HOOK_PAYOFF_GAP (over-delivery, r=-0.52), END_RECOVERY (80-95% zone, r=+0.506), and MOMENTUM_ZONES (r=+0.468).

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.
top_indicators.why: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
top_indicators.why: the proof MOMENT is an action frame — visible build/test/body/counter state in one shot
single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.filter: factual premise fields × endpoint kind — each driver exposes the matched verb/anchor/artifact and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: endpoint is experiment/identity and no single-frame artifact is named — inverse of HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + inverse of end_begin_ratio (payoff reads only with context)
HANDS_VISIBLE match in visual_action_short / first_frame_action — indicator_registry.visual_is_workshop axis (hands-on-object framing)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

top_indicators.quantification: Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops
top_indicators.why: frame-1 comprehensibility IS an action frame at t=0s — action verb in first_frame_action is the opening-second proxy for this cause
visible body_part_phrase (foot/shoulders/stomach/hand) has a surface to film; feeling/head/mind/brain/memory do not
title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (I got called fat so I became a BODYBUILDER) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 7 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=-0.32 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.52 from 4 drivers (+4/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

ideas.title: Can this $20,000 laser cut through my ARM?
ideas.one_line_premise: I put my arm in front of a high-powered laser, increase the exposure in controlled steps, and keep filming until the test gives a real answer inside a workshop test rig with visible safety barriers and a live power readout, narrating every sensation in my arm until the laser either cuts through my arm or it visibly doesn’t, and the camera freezes on the real result.

concept.title: Can this $20,000 laser cut through my ARM?
concept.logline: I put my arm in front of a high-powered laser, increase the exposure in controlled steps, and keep filming until the test gives a real answer inside a workshop test rig with visible safety barriers and a live power readout, narrating every sensation in my arm until the laser either cuts through my arm or it visibly doesn’t, and the camera freezes on the real result.
concept.promise: You're watching me put my arm in front of a high-powered laser, increase the exposure in controlled steps, and keep filming until the test gives a real answer — the question is where the laser gives a visible answer on my arm.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the laser either cuts through my arm or it visibly doesn’t, and the camera freezes on the real result; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers the laser either cuts through my arm or it visibly doesn’t, and the camera freezes on the real result as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on the laser head powering on while my arm and the safety rig are both clearly in frame. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my arm is going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on my arm moving toward the laser path with the power setting visible on screen for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter inside a workshop test rig with visible safety barriers and a live power readout; my arm moving toward the laser path with the power setting visible on screen; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first arm sensation narration; counter begins to tick; my arm moving toward the laser path with the power setting visible on screen
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating my arm moving toward the laser path with the power setting visible on screen with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the laser either cuts through my arm or it visibly doesn’t, and the camera freezes on the real result; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the laser either cuts through my arm or it visibly doesn’t, and the camera freezes on the real result; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with arm + impact word: "arm's numb — insane."

first_5s: the laser head powering on while my arm and the safety rig are both clearly in frame
hook_quarter: my arm moving toward the laser path with the power setting visible on screen
mid: alternate arm close-ups with my arm moving toward the laser path with the power setting visible on screen
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

drivers.source: findings.kept_signals.pat_making_v2 (delta_r2=+0.012; 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M) + proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos)
top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
title_making_keyword ($24M avg for build/test format) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot before/after or build-until-fail visible payoff)

creator_fit.note: Creator-fit is added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector rewards maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level. Weights derived from indicator strengths above — no hand-curated creator taste beyond what the corpus already implies.

drivers.source: proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos) + top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot visible payoff)
wave11_12.end_begin_ratio — qualitative reveal is single-shot legible when premise copy names a concrete artifact (scored in D)

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frames ARE the proof moment)
proof_clarity.note: Proof-clarity rewards single-shot legible payoffs (build+test hybrids, body before/after anchors, numeric counter freeze + named artifact) and penalizes observation/identity endpoints with no artifact, cognitive/head-framed payoffs, and stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. Added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector and final re-rank both reward mechanism visibility at the concrete premise level. No hand-picked top-5 — every weight reads a factual premise field and cites a corpus indicator.

drivers.source: body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in premise copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

endpoint is numeric/body/build_test AND the premise has no builtin reveal — composeTitle() appends a single-frame freeze ("The Counter Froze At N" / "The Timer Hit N Exactly" / "The Mile Counter Froze At N" / "My {part} Quit First" / "The Build Held Until N"); wave11_12.end_begin_ratio
first_frame_action names an action verb + at least one of {gauge, physical object} — the challenge is legible at t=0s; HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause applied at the opening

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frame at t=0s IS comprehension)
visual_legibility.note: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No abstract classification layer — the six axes are factual premise fields plus the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.
top_indicators.why: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.

top_indicators.quantification: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.
top_indicators.rule: Put your weakest/setup content in the first 20%. Never let the worst moment happen after 30%.
top_indicators.why: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.

top_indicators.quantification: phrases like "link in bio" / "see what happens" / "on my profile" are enriched at drops
top_indicators.rule: avoid drop phrases; prefer peak phrases ("in the comments", "should I keep", "i keep going")
top_indicators.why: phrases like "link in bio" / "see what happens" / "on my profile" are enriched at drops

top_indicators.rule: avoid stakes-based framing ("if this fails…") — use transformation or mystery
verbal energy drives retention more than visual. Pair visual beats with spoken sensory language.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

creator_fit.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass and MMR fill both reward maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
top_indicators.why: the proof MOMENT is an action frame — visible build/test/body/counter state in one shot
single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.filter: factual premise fields × endpoint kind — each driver exposes the matched verb/anchor/artifact and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos) + top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot visible payoff)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (Can this $20,000 laser cut through my ARM?) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 8 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.52 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.48 from 4 drivers (+4/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

ideas.title: Can this stop a bullet?
ideas.one_line_premise: I test whether different homemade materials can stop a bullet by escalating through real shots until the answer is visually undeniable at an outdoor ballistic range with the target rig and impact camera both visible, narrating every sensation in my hands until the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result.

concept.title: Can this stop a bullet?
concept.logline: I test whether different homemade materials can stop a bullet by escalating through real shots until the answer is visually undeniable at an outdoor ballistic range with the target rig and impact camera both visible, narrating every sensation in my hands until the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result.
concept.promise: You're watching me test whether different homemade materials can stop a bullet by escalating through real shots until the answer is visually undeniable — the question is where different homemade materials either stops the bullet or visibly fails.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on the target rig, impact zone, and projectile test setup all framed together before the first shot. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my hands are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter an outdoor ballistic range with the target rig and impact camera both visible; a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first hands sensation narration; counter begins to tick; a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the bullet either gets stopped by different homemade materials or it visibly punches through, and the impact frame freezes on the real result; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with hands + impact word: "hands's numb — insane."

first_5s: the target rig, impact zone, and projectile test setup all framed together before the first shot
hook_quarter: a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen
mid: alternate hands close-ups with a projectile test hitting the target while the impact result is frozen on screen
text overlay at beat moments only

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

build_phases.rationale: Build phases are validated against the top-5 retention predictors + narrative_arc_analysis (best/worst arc labels) + wave7 retention-shape evidence.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

build_phases.filter: retention-curve predictors ranked by |r_with_views| across the video pool

worst_arc.description: Front-loaded content that loses viewers progressively. 10x worse than steady_rise.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.
top_indicators.why: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

top_indicators.quantification: Title contains making/build/creat/construct — 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M

top_indicators.why: highest-value single title-word pattern in corpus

top_indicators.quantification: Video shot in workshop-like environment (42/203 positive, 2.6x)
Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops

top_indicators.why: single-shot visible payoff structure rewards transformation/build endings

creator_fit.filter: indicators with published r/r_partial/delta_r2 in the evidence lattice above a minimum effect threshold

drivers_triggered.source: findings.kept_signals.pat_making_v2 (delta_r2=+0.012; 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M) + proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos)
top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
title_making_keyword ($24M avg for build/test format) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot before/after or build-until-fail visible payoff)

creator_fit.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass and MMR fill both reward maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
top_indicators.why: the proof MOMENT is an action frame — visible build/test/body/counter state in one shot
single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.filter: factual premise fields × endpoint kind — each driver exposes the matched verb/anchor/artifact and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos) + top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot visible payoff)
wave11_12.end_begin_ratio — qualitative reveal is single-shot legible when premise copy names a concrete artifact (scored in D)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

top_indicators.quantification: Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops
top_indicators.why: frame-1 comprehensibility IS an action frame at t=0s — action verb in first_frame_action is the opening-second proxy for this cause
visible body_part_phrase (foot/shoulders/stomach/hand) has a surface to film; feeling/head/mind/brain/memory do not
title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

top_indicators.quantification: Title contains making/build/creat/construct — 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M
top_indicators.why: title_making_keyword pattern aligns with physical reveal phrasing

visual_legibility.filter: six factual premise axes + title string — each driver exposes the matched stem/phrase/body-part and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in premise copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

endpoint is numeric/body/build_test AND the premise has no builtin reveal — composeTitle() appends a single-frame freeze ("The Counter Froze At N" / "The Timer Hit N Exactly" / "The Mile Counter Froze At N" / "My {part} Quit First" / "The Build Held Until N"); wave11_12.end_begin_ratio
first_frame_action names an action verb + at least one of {gauge, physical object} — the challenge is legible at t=0s; HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause applied at the opening

visual_legibility.selection_effect: Added to the combo score alongside proof_clarity so the diversity-aware selector, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all push cognitive/abstract/verbal-reveal premises below zero before any top-5 slot is assigned.
