summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (Can this stop a bullet?) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 7 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.52 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.48 from 4 drivers (+4/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

ideas.title: Making a fire proof shield
ideas.one_line_premise: I build a FIREPROOF shield from heat-resistant materials in my workshop, then expose it to escalating fire until the real failure point (or total survival) becomes impossible to dispute in an open outdoor test area with full fire kit visible and the shield centred on the test stand, narrating every sensation in my hands until the fire either overwhelms the FIREPROOF shield on camera or the build holds through every escalating test, and the camera freezes on the frame that settles it.

concept.title: Making a fire proof shield
concept.logline: I build a FIREPROOF shield from heat-resistant materials in my workshop, then expose it to escalating fire until the real failure point (or total survival) becomes impossible to dispute in an open outdoor test area with full fire kit visible and the shield centred on the test stand, narrating every sensation in my hands until the fire either overwhelms the FIREPROOF shield on camera or the build holds through every escalating test, and the camera freezes on the frame that settles it.
concept.promise: You're watching me build a FIREPROOF shield from heat-resistant materials in my workshop, then expose it to escalating fire until the real failure point (or total survival) becomes impossible to dispute — the question is where the fire forces a visible verdict on the FIREPROOF shield.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the fire either overwhelms the FIREPROOF shield on camera or the build holds through every escalating test, and the camera freezes on the frame that settles it; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers the fire either overwhelms the FIREPROOF shield on camera or the build holds through every escalating test, and the camera freezes on the frame that settles it as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on the finished FIREPROOF shield on a test stand with a flamethrower visible in the background, both in the same frame before the first flame. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my hands are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on flames hitting the shield surface while the camera holds close on the material — every blister, char mark, and smoke curl visible for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter an open outdoor test area with full fire kit visible and the shield centred on the test stand; flames hitting the shield surface while the camera holds close on the material — every blister, char mark, and smoke curl visible; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first hands sensation narration; counter begins to tick; flames hitting the shield surface while the camera holds close on the material — every blister, char mark, and smoke curl visible
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating flames hitting the shield surface while the camera holds close on the material — every blister, char mark, and smoke curl visible with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the fire either overwhelms the FIREPROOF shield on camera or the build holds through every escalating test, and the camera freezes on the frame that settles it; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the fire either overwhelms the FIREPROOF shield on camera or the build holds through every escalating test, and the camera freezes on the frame that settles it; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with hands + impact word: "hands's numb — insane."

first_5s: the finished FIREPROOF shield on a test stand with a flamethrower visible in the background, both in the same frame before the first flame
hook_quarter: flames hitting the shield surface while the camera holds close on the material — every blister, char mark, and smoke curl visible
mid: alternate hands close-ups with flames hitting the shield surface while the camera holds close on the material — every blister, char mark, and smoke curl visible
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

drivers.source: findings.kept_signals.pat_making_v2 (delta_r2=+0.012; 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M) + proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos)
top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
title_making_keyword ($24M avg for build/test format) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot before/after or build-until-fail visible payoff)

creator_fit.note: Creator-fit is added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector rewards maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level. Weights derived from indicator strengths above — no hand-curated creator taste beyond what the corpus already implies.

drivers.source: proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos) + top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot visible payoff)
wave11_12.end_begin_ratio — qualitative reveal is single-shot legible when premise copy names a concrete artifact (scored in D)

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frames ARE the proof moment)
proof_clarity.note: Proof-clarity rewards single-shot legible payoffs (build+test hybrids, body before/after anchors, numeric counter freeze + named artifact) and penalizes observation/identity endpoints with no artifact, cognitive/head-framed payoffs, and stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. Added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector and final re-rank both reward mechanism visibility at the concrete premise level. No hand-picked top-5 — every weight reads a factual premise field and cites a corpus indicator.

drivers.source: body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in premise copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

endpoint is numeric/body/build_test AND the premise has no builtin reveal — composeTitle() appends a single-frame freeze ("The Counter Froze At N" / "The Timer Hit N Exactly" / "The Mile Counter Froze At N" / "My {part} Quit First" / "The Build Held Until N"); wave11_12.end_begin_ratio
first_frame_action lacks an action verb, a gauge, AND a physical object — viewer cannot parse the challenge from the first frame; inverse of HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frame at t=0s IS comprehension)
visual_legibility.note: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No abstract classification layer — the six axes are factual premise fields plus the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

build_phases.filter: retention-curve predictors ranked by |r_with_views| across the video pool

best_arc.description: 54% of all videos follow this arc. Consistently building above_baseline from hook to end.

worst_arc.description: Front-loaded content that loses viewers progressively. 10x worse than steady_rise.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.
top_indicators.why: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.

top_indicators.quantification: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
top_indicators.why: the proof MOMENT is an action frame — visible build/test/body/counter state in one shot
single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.filter: factual premise fields × endpoint kind — each driver exposes the matched verb/anchor/artifact and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos) + top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot visible payoff)
wave11_12.end_begin_ratio — qualitative reveal is single-shot legible when premise copy names a concrete artifact (scored in D)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

first_frame_action lacks an action verb, a gauge, AND a physical object — viewer cannot parse the challenge from the first frame; inverse of HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (Making a fire proof shield) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 8 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.52 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.26 from 4 drivers (+3/-1) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

ideas.title: Making the coolest Halloween costume ever
ideas.one_line_premise: I design and build the most elaborate the character costume possible in my workshop, testing each component piece on camera, then wear the complete costume in a real public setting to capture the live reaction in my workshop for the build, then out in a real public location wearing the finished costume for the reveal, narrating every sensation in my hands until the completed costume lands in a real public setting and the camera holds on the first genuine reaction from someone who didn't know it was coming.

concept.title: Making the coolest Halloween costume ever
concept.logline: I design and build the most elaborate the character costume possible in my workshop, testing each component piece on camera, then wear the complete costume in a real public setting to capture the live reaction in my workshop for the build, then out in a real public location wearing the finished costume for the reveal, narrating every sensation in my hands until the completed costume lands in a real public setting and the camera holds on the first genuine reaction from someone who didn't know it was coming.
concept.promise: You're watching me design and build the most elaborate the character costume possible in my workshop, testing each component piece on camera, then wear the complete costume in a real public setting to capture the live reaction — the question is where the finished costume meets a real public setting and the reaction lands.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the completed costume lands in a real public setting and the camera holds on the first genuine reaction from someone who didn't know it was coming; a single word of reaction lands as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers the completed costume lands in a real public setting and the camera holds on the first genuine reaction from someone who didn't know it was coming as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on the full completed costume on a workshop mannequin surrounded by the build tools, every piece locked in, before I step into frame and put it on. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my hands are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on the costume being assembled and test-fitted piece by piece with each component's reaction captured in the same frame as the build for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter my workshop for the build, then out in a real public location wearing the finished costume for the reveal; the costume being assembled and test-fitted piece by piece with each component's reaction captured in the same frame as the build; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first hands sensation narration; counter begins to tick; the costume being assembled and test-fitted piece by piece with each component's reaction captured in the same frame as the build
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating the costume being assembled and test-fitted piece by piece with each component's reaction captured in the same frame as the build with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the completed costume lands in a real public setting and the camera holds on the first genuine reaction from someone who didn't know it was coming; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the completed costume lands in a real public setting and the camera holds on the first genuine reaction from someone who didn't know it was coming; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with hands + impact word: "hands's numb — insane."

first_5s: the full completed costume on a workshop mannequin surrounded by the build tools, every piece locked in, before I step into frame and put it on
hook_quarter: the costume being assembled and test-fitted piece by piece with each component's reaction captured in the same frame as the build
mid: alternate hands close-ups with the costume being assembled and test-fitted piece by piece with each component's reaction captured in the same frame as the build
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

Added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector and final re-rank both reward mechanism visibility at the concrete premise level. No hand-picked top-5 — every weight reads a factual premise field and cites a corpus indicator.

visual_legibility.note: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No abstract classification layer — the six axes are factual premise fields plus the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

top_indicators.rule: NEVER name materials. Show the result, not the process. 'Watch what happens' > 'I used carbon fiber reinforced with graphene layers'.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.
top_indicators.why: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.

top_indicators.quantification: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.
top_indicators.rule: Put your weakest/setup content in the first 20%. Never let the worst moment happen after 30%.
top_indicators.why: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure

object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

endpoint is experiment/identity and no single-frame artifact is named — inverse of HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + inverse of end_begin_ratio (payoff reads only with context)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

why_it_works: Specific premise (Making the coolest Halloween costume ever) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 7 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.22 from 2 drivers (+2/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

ideas.title: Making INDESTRUCTIBLE armour
ideas.one_line_premise: I build a piece of INDESTRUCTIBLE armour from scratch, layer the reinforcement until the build feels wrong to destroy, then test it against escalating forces on camera until it either holds or visibly fails in my workshop with the finished armour and all test tools visible before the first hit, narrating every sensation in my hands until the INDESTRUCTIBLE armour either survives every test or one specific hit visibly ends it, and the camera freezes on the real result.

concept.title: Making INDESTRUCTIBLE armour
concept.logline: I build a piece of INDESTRUCTIBLE armour from scratch, layer the reinforcement until the build feels wrong to destroy, then test it against escalating forces on camera until it either holds or visibly fails in my workshop with the finished armour and all test tools visible before the first hit, narrating every sensation in my hands until the INDESTRUCTIBLE armour either survives every test or one specific hit visibly ends it, and the camera freezes on the real result.
concept.promise: You're watching me build a piece of INDESTRUCTIBLE armour from scratch, layer the reinforcement until the build feels wrong to destroy, then test it against escalating forces on camera until it either holds or visibly fails — the question is where the INDESTRUCTIBLE armour gets a visible verdict from the escalating test.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the INDESTRUCTIBLE armour either survives every test or one specific hit visibly ends it, and the camera freezes on the real result; a single word of reaction lands as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers the INDESTRUCTIBLE armour either survives every test or one specific hit visibly ends it, and the camera freezes on the real result as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on the finished INDESTRUCTIBLE armour on the workshop table with the test tools — hammers, weights, fire — arranged around it before the first impact. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my hands are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on hammers and weights hitting the armour while the camera holds tight on every impact point and dent for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter my workshop with the finished armour and all test tools visible before the first hit; hammers and weights hitting the armour while the camera holds tight on every impact point and dent; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first hands sensation narration; counter begins to tick; hammers and weights hitting the armour while the camera holds tight on every impact point and dent
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating hammers and weights hitting the armour while the camera holds tight on every impact point and dent with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the INDESTRUCTIBLE armour either survives every test or one specific hit visibly ends it, and the camera freezes on the real result; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the INDESTRUCTIBLE armour either survives every test or one specific hit visibly ends it, and the camera freezes on the real result; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with hands + impact word: "hands's numb — insane."

first_5s: the finished INDESTRUCTIBLE armour on the workshop table with the test tools — hammers, weights, fire — arranged around it before the first impact
hook_quarter: hammers and weights hitting the armour while the camera holds tight on every impact point and dent
mid: alternate hands close-ups with hammers and weights hitting the armour while the camera holds tight on every impact point and dent

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

creator_fit.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass and MMR fill both reward maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
top_indicators.why: the proof MOMENT is an action frame — visible build/test/body/counter state in one shot
single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.filter: factual premise fields × endpoint kind — each driver exposes the matched verb/anchor/artifact and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: proven_discoveries.title_making_keyword ($24M avg, 23 videos) + top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot visible payoff)
endpoint is experiment/identity and no single-frame artifact is named — inverse of HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + inverse of end_begin_ratio (payoff reads only with context)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

top_indicators.why: frame-1 comprehensibility IS an action frame at t=0s — action verb in first_frame_action is the opening-second proxy for this cause

title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

physical-action verb stem present in premise copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES
first_frame_action lacks an action verb, a gauge, AND a physical object — viewer cannot parse the challenge from the first frame; inverse of HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (Making INDESTRUCTIBLE armour) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 7 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.1 from 3 drivers (+2/-1) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

evidence_summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

ideas.title: Breaking a world record
ideas.one_line_premise: I chase a real world record attempt from setup to failure or success, keeping the counter and the emotional swing visible the whole time inside the record attempt setup with the goal number and attempt both visible, narrating every sensation in my hands until the record attempt lands on whatever the real final number turns out to be with the counter still in frame.

concept.title: Breaking a world record
concept.logline: I chase a real world record attempt from setup to failure or success, keeping the counter and the emotional swing visible the whole time inside the record attempt setup with the goal number and attempt both visible, narrating every sensation in my hands until the record attempt lands on whatever the real final number turns out to be with the counter still in frame.
concept.promise: You're watching me chase a real world record attempt from setup to failure or success, keeping the counter and the emotional swing visible the whole time — the question is where the world record attempt lands on its real final number.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the record attempt lands on whatever the real final number turns out to be with the counter still in frame; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers the record attempt lands on whatever the real final number turns out to be with the counter still in frame as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on the record setup and the number to beat both visible before the first attempt starts. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my hands are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on each attempt tightening while the target number stays on screen for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter inside the record attempt setup with the goal number and attempt both visible; each attempt tightening while the target number stays on screen; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first hands sensation narration; counter begins to tick; each attempt tightening while the target number stays on screen
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating each attempt tightening while the target number stays on screen with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the record attempt lands on whatever the real final number turns out to be with the counter still in frame; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the record attempt lands on whatever the real final number turns out to be with the counter still in frame; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with hands + impact word: "hands's numb — insane."

first_5s: the record setup and the number to beat both visible before the first attempt starts
hook_quarter: each attempt tightening while the target number stays on screen
mid: alternate hands close-ups with each attempt tightening while the target number stays on screen
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

creator_fit.note: Creator-fit is added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector rewards maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level. Weights derived from indicator strengths above — no hand-curated creator taste beyond what the corpus already implies.

drivers.source: first_frame_action / visual_action_short names a physical artifact or overlay — HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause (artifact visible in the proof shot)

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frames ARE the proof moment)
proof_clarity.note: Proof-clarity rewards single-shot legible payoffs (build+test hybrids, body before/after anchors, numeric counter freeze + named artifact) and penalizes observation/identity endpoints with no artifact, cognitive/head-framed payoffs, and stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. Added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector and final re-rank both reward mechanism visibility at the concrete premise level. No hand-picked top-5 — every weight reads a factual premise field and cites a corpus indicator.

drivers.source: body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in premise copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

endpoint is numeric/body/build_test AND the premise has no builtin reveal — composeTitle() appends a single-frame freeze ("The Counter Froze At N" / "The Timer Hit N Exactly" / "The Mile Counter Froze At N" / "My {part} Quit First" / "The Build Held Until N"); wave11_12.end_begin_ratio
first_frame_action names an action verb + at least one of {gauge, physical object} — the challenge is legible at t=0s; HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause applied at the opening

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frame at t=0s IS comprehension)
visual_legibility.note: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No abstract classification layer — the six axes are factual premise fields plus the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

build_phases.rationale: Build phases are validated against the top-5 retention predictors + narrative_arc_analysis (best/worst arc labels) + wave7 retention-shape evidence.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

build_phases.filter: retention-curve predictors ranked by |r_with_views| across the video pool

best_arc.description: 54% of all videos follow this arc. Consistently building above_baseline from hook to end.

worst_arc.description: Front-loaded content that loses viewers progressively. 10x worse than steady_rise.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)

object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (Breaking a world record) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 8 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.12 from 1 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.48 from 4 drivers (+4/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

ideas.title: Running a marathon in BANNED shoes
ideas.one_line_premise: I run a full marathon in banned racing shoes and keep filming each physical change until my feet make the cost obvious on a live marathon course with the race clock and road markings visible, narrating every sensation in my feet until my feet hit the exact moment they stop cooperating with the banned shoes on the marathon course.

concept.title: Running a marathon in BANNED shoes
concept.logline: I run a full marathon in banned racing shoes and keep filming each physical change until my feet make the cost obvious on a live marathon course with the race clock and road markings visible, narrating every sensation in my feet until my feet hit the exact moment they stop cooperating with the banned shoes on the marathon course.
concept.promise: You're watching me run a full marathon in banned racing shoes and keep filming each physical change until my feet make the cost obvious — the question is where my feet decide how far the banned shoes can carry me.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: my feet hit the exact moment they stop cooperating with the banned shoes on the marathon course; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook is the exact validated title tease; payoff delivers my feet hit the exact moment they stop cooperating with the banned shoes on the marathon course as the single visible on-camera moment that resolves it — the resolved frame over-delivers vs the headline question.

opening.first_frame: Close on lacing up the banned shoes next to the start line with the race clock already visible. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — my feet are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on the shoes pounding pavement while the race clock and stride stay in frame for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter a live marathon course with the race clock and road markings visible; the shoes pounding pavement while the race clock and stride stay in frame; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first feet sensation narration; counter begins to tick; the shoes pounding pavement while the race clock and stride stay in frame
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating the shoes pounding pavement while the race clock and stride stay in frame with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: my feet hit the exact moment they stop cooperating with the banned shoes on the marathon course; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, my feet hit the exact moment they stop cooperating with the banned shoes on the marathon course; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with feet + impact word: "feet's numb — insane."

hook_quarter: the shoes pounding pavement while the race clock and stride stay in frame
mid: alternate feet close-ups with the shoes pounding pavement while the race clock and stride stay in frame
text overlay at beat moments only

It reads the concrete premise along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No abstract classification layer — the six axes are factual premise fields plus the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.
top_indicators.why: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

top_indicators.quantification: Title contains making/build/creat/construct — 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M

top_indicators.why: highest-value single title-word pattern in corpus

top_indicators.quantification: Video shot in workshop-like environment (42/203 positive, 2.6x)
Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops

top_indicators.why: single-shot visible payoff structure rewards transformation/build endings

creator_fit.filter: indicators with published r/r_partial/delta_r2 in the evidence lattice above a minimum effect threshold

drivers_triggered.source: inverse of visual_is_workshop (r=+0.236); HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause favors tactile fixed framing over roaming outdoor
top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (numeric counter freeze = single-shot visible payoff)

creator_fit.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass and MMR fill both reward maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
top_indicators.why: the proof MOMENT is an action frame — visible build/test/body/counter state in one shot
single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.filter: factual premise fields × endpoint kind — each driver exposes the matched verb/anchor/artifact and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio — numeric counter freeze is single-shot legible proof
first_frame_action / visual_action_short names a physical artifact or overlay — HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause (artifact visible in the proof shot)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the concrete premise on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch premises that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no abstract classification layer; no hand-picked top 5.

top_indicators.quantification: Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops
top_indicators.why: frame-1 comprehensibility IS an action frame at t=0s — action verb in first_frame_action is the opening-second proxy for this cause
visible body_part_phrase (foot/shoulders/stomach/hand) has a surface to film; feeling/head/mind/brain/memory do not
title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

top_indicators.quantification: Title contains making/build/creat/construct — 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M
top_indicators.why: title_making_keyword pattern aligns with physical reveal phrasing

visual_legibility.filter: six factual premise axes + title string — each driver exposes the matched stem/phrase/body-part and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in premise copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

endpoint is numeric/body/build_test AND the premise has no builtin reveal — composeTitle() appends a single-frame freeze ("The Counter Froze At N" / "The Timer Hit N Exactly" / "The Mile Counter Froze At N" / "My {part} Quit First" / "The Build Held Until N"); wave11_12.end_begin_ratio

visual_action_short names a state→state change (cut between, day1 vs dayN, growing stack/pile/tower, slides out, pedals turning, mile-counter) — wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (legible progress / before-after)

visual_legibility.selection_effect: Added to the combo score alongside proof_clarity so the diversity-aware selector, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all push cognitive/abstract/verbal-reveal premises below zero before any top-5 slot is assigned.

hook_retention_20s.rationale: Additive model: corpus mean retention@20s (79.3%) + design deltas keyed to top peak/drop causes and opening density.

top_indicators.why: corpus mean = 79.3%; r_with_views ≈ 0.60 (single strongest view predictor)

share_propensity.rationale: Additive model: nominal 3% share baseline + peak-phrase triggers, shareable hook taxonomy, golden final 5% payoff, and penalty for CTA-drift phrases.

keep_rate.rationale: Additive model: corpus mean keep + deltas from over-delivery structure, monotonic rise, and nadir placement — each grounded in an explicit retention-pattern signal.

top_indicators.quantification: median views when nadir precedes climax

view_band.rationale: Design score is mapped onto the video-scorecard total-score quintiles (views_median, p25, p75) and then expanded by the full-model prediction-range multiplier to express residual uncertainty.

top_indicators.quantification: Multiplier used to widen quintile band for uncertainty

view_band.filter: scorecard total-score quintiles (view_median, p25, p75) × full-model range multiplier
view_band.note: MODELED — not a prediction. 72.9% of view variance is external (algorithm, timing, audience).

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (Running a marathon in BANNED shoes) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 8 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.34 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.46 from 4 drivers (+4/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).
